description: Comprehensive web application penetration testing exam focusing on content discovery, authentication issues, IDOR, SQLi/XSS/SSRF, and Burp Suite workflows.

scenarios.description: Corporate web application with multiple vulnerability classes
objectives: Discover hidden directories and admin panels
Test for IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)
Exploit SQL injection to extract credentials
Find user flag in web application
Escalate privileges and find root flag

hints.strategic: Web applications often expose functionality through hidden directories. Use wordlists like directory-list-2.3-medium.txt or raft-medium-directories.txt.
hints.tactical: Common admin panel paths: /admin, /administrator, /console, /dashboard, /panel. Look for backup files (.bak, .old, ~) and configuration files.
hints.technical: Burp Suite's Repeater is essential for manual SQLi testing. Try single quotes (') in URL parameters and form fields to test for injection points.
hints.specific: The /api/users endpoint may have IDOR. Try incrementing user IDs: /api/users/1, /api/users/2. Authentication tokens might be predictable or bypassable.

sqliDetection: Burp Suite Intruder with SQLi payloads
idorExploitation: Burp Suite Intruder to enumerate object IDs
authenticationBypass: SQL injection in login: admin' OR '1'='1'-- -
Test for default credentials: admin/admin, admin/password
Session cookie analysis in Burp Suite

commandCommentary.gobuster: Industry-standard directory fuzzer. Flags: -u (URL), -w (wordlist), -x (file extensions), -t (threads). Fast and reliable for content discovery.
commandCommentary.sqlmap: Automated SQL injection tool. Flags: -u (URL), --batch (non-interactive), --dbs (enumerate databases), --dump (extract data). Can be noisy - use carefully in real engagements.
commandCommentary.burpsuite: Essential web proxy. Repeater for manual testing, Intruder for automation, Scanner for discovery. PT1 focuses on manual techniques, not automated scanning.
commandCommentary.ffuf: Fast web fuzzer written in Go. More flexible than gobuster - can fuzz headers, POST data, multiple positions. Recommended for advanced fuzzing.
commandCommentary.wfuzz: Powerful web fuzzer. Can fuzz any part of HTTP request. Use --hc (hide response codes) to filter results.
commandCommentary.curl: Command-line HTTP client. Essential for manual testing. Flags: -X (method), -H (headers), -d (POST data), -i (show headers), -k (ignore SSL errors).

kaliAlternatives.sqlmap: Manual SQLi testing with curl/Burp, nosqlmap (for NoSQL)
